title: polygala flavescens subsp. maremmana
intro: Polygala flavescens subsp. maremmana, also known as Yellow Milkwort, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Polygalaceae family, native to the Mediterranean region. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, which help to alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The health benefits of Yellow Milkwort include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant activity. In herbal preparations, Yellow Milkwort is often used as a tea or tincture to treat respiratory issues, and its dried roots can be used in powder form to make capsules or add to other herbal blends.
